app.name: 360 Render
app.description: Private 360 panorama render studio for architects and interior designers.
app.currentMvp: A Next.js app with a public marketing homepage, Supabase email/password auth protecting a project gallery and immersive 360 panorama viewer, and server-side render pipeline endpoints prepared for OpenAI image edits, Topaz upscaling, and Supabase storage.

techStack.auth: Supabase Auth through @supabase/ssr cookie-based server-side clients and Next.js proxy route protection
techStack.storage: Supabase Storage through a server-only admin client
techStack.imageRendering: OpenAI Images API via server-only helper
techStack.upscaling: Topaz Labs Image API via server-only helper

appShell.responsibility: Defines app metadata, imports global CSS, and renders the root HTML/body wrapper.

productDesignContext.responsibility: Strategic design context for the public brand surface: register, audience, product purpose, brand personality, anti-references, principles, and accessibility notes.

publicRoot.responsibility: Public homepage with dark glassmorphism product positioning, hero proof points, panorama viewer preview, feature cards, workflow steps, showcase, pricing, footer CTAs, anchor links, and placeholder action buttons.

homeInteractions.responsibility: Client-side wrapper for homepage-only placeholder actions, newsletter submit prevention, and toast-style click feedback without navigating away.

loginPage.responsibility: Email/password login and signup form using Supabase Auth server actions.

loginActions.responsibility: Server actions for signInWithPassword and signUp, including safe next-path redirects and signup email redirect metadata.

authConfirmRoute.responsibility: Handles Supabase email confirmation token_hash callbacks and redirects confirmed users into the protected workspace.

workspaceRoutes.responsibility: Protected App Router pages that mount WorkspaceApp on the Projects or Viewer screen.

workspaceClientExperience.responsibility: Client-side project gallery and panorama viewer UI. Maintains local navigation state, syncs viewer state to /projects and /viewer query params, and exposes logout.

heroPanoramaPreview.responsibility: Client-side interactive panorama preview on the public homepage, mounting PanoramaSphere with the generated 2:1 penthouse panorama plus title overlay and working reset, zoom, detail, and fullscreen controls.

authProxy.responsibility: Refreshes Supabase SSR auth cookies and redirects guests away from /projects and /viewer while redirecting logged-in users away from /login.

supabaseSsrClients.responsibility: Browser and per-request server Supabase clients for cookie-based Auth sessions. Server client falls back to existing non-public env names for local compatibility.

globalStyles.responsibility: Dark desktop-style app chrome, monochrome glass public homepage layout and motion, placeholder action toast styling, project grid/list layouts, viewer overlays, login form styling, responsive behavior, and entrance animations.

panoramaViewer.responsibility: Dynamically imports Photo Sphere Viewer on the client, creates/destroys the viewer instance, exposes a minimal controller, and falls back to a static image when WebGL is unavailable.

renderJobsApi.responsibility: Accepts multipart render-job requests. Mock mode returns a fake job. Real mode uploads the source image, renders through OpenAI, stores the OpenAI output, starts a Topaz upscale, and returns provider/storage metadata.

topazStatusApi.responsibility: Polls Topaz async status by process id and includes a download link once the Topaz job is completed.

supabaseServer.responsibility: Server-only Supabase admin client singleton using SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY.

panoramaStorage.responsibility: Uploads panorama assets to the configured Supabase Storage bucket and creates signed URLs for stored assets.

domainLibraries.responsibility: Render status types, panorama validation, render presets, and richer mock data that is still only partially wired into the visible workspace.

supabaseSchema.responsibility: Planned persistence model for projects, scenes, render jobs, private storage bucket, and owner-based row level security policies.

frontendArchitecture.renderingModel: The protected workspace is a client component because it uses local state, browser URL APIs, and imperative viewer controls. Login and route protection are server-rendered/server-action driven.

routes.behavior: Public homepage with Start Free and pricing CTAs linking to /login, Watch Demo anchoring to the workflow section, and View All Projects linking to protected /projects.
Email/password login and signup form. Logged-in users are redirected to /projects by proxy.
Protected 360 panorama viewer opened from a project card or direct query param.

screens.name: Homepage
features: brand navigation with Pricing, Showcase, Log in, and Start Free links
dark glass hero with Start Free and Watch Demo CTAs, proof points, and interactive 360 panorama preview
Render, Upscale, and Present feature cards
4-step workflow section for upload, preset selection, render/upscale, and viewer sharing
showcase project cards using local panorama assets and reference-aligned project labels
static pricing section with Free, Pro, and Studio plans, Yearly selected, and Save up to 30% billing UI
placeholder footer, social, billing, legal, and newsletter actions with in-page feedback
monochrome dark gray and off-black glass CSS treatment with soft light beams, panorama imagery, transform/opacity entrance motion, hover/press motion, and reduced-motion handling

screens.name: Projects
features: top bar with Projects and Viewer tabs
project cards with local static panorama thumbnails

navigation.type: Local React state with route/query-string persistence

assets.panoramaImages: public/panoramas/*.jpg plus public/panoramas/generated-hero-panorama.png for the homepage viewer preview

apiRoutes.input: Supabase token_hash, type, and optional next query params
apiRoutes.behavior: Verifies the OTP token through Supabase Auth and redirects to the protected workspace or login error state.

apiRoutes.input: multipart/form-data with image File, presetId string, optional notes string, and optional mode string
apiRoutes.mockBehavior: Any mode other than real returns a fake job id with status rendering_openai and skips provider calls.
realBehavior: upload source asset to Supabase Storage
call OpenAI image edits with the selected render preset
upload OpenAI render output to Supabase Storage
return job, storage, OpenAI, and Topaz metadata

apiRoutes.input: Topaz process id in the route segment
apiRoutes.behavior: Fetches Topaz status and adds download metadata when status is completed.

providerFlow: Authenticated user submits a 2:1 panorama image and render preset.
Server stores the source in Supabase Storage.
Server sends the image and prompt to OpenAI Images for a 3840x1920 jpeg render.
Server stores the OpenAI render in Supabase Storage.
Server sends the OpenAI render to Topaz for a 4096x2048 jpeg upscale.
Client can poll Topaz status and request download metadata when completed.
securityBoundaries: Provider API keys and Supabase secret key are only read from server-only modules or API routes.
Browser/client auth uses only NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY.
Supabase SSR proxy uses getClaims to validate protected workspace access from cookies.
Supabase admin client uses disabled session persistence and token refresh.
Supabase schema enables row level security for projects, scenes, and render_jobs.
Storage bucket is private by default.

domainModel.authUsers: Supabase Auth manages user accounts; no custom public users table exists in this MVP.

files.coverage: Source-level checks for public homepage content and dark glass styling hooks, hero panorama preview asset, auth route structure, protected workspace routes, proxy guard, login actions, env documentation, and architecture docs.
Render status progress and mock state transitions.
Panorama file validation for supported types, dimensions, and 2:1 ratio.
Source-level regression checks that the current workspace does not expose removed scenes rail or jobs screen UI.

knownGaps: The visible workspace still uses local project data rather than persisted Supabase project rows.
The current visible UI does not yet wire upload, preset selection, notes, render-job creation, polling, or persistence into the app screen.
src/lib/mock-data.ts contains richer project and scene concepts that are not currently imported by WorkspaceApp.
Real provider error handling is basic and bubbles thrown errors from API routes.
Topaz final asset upload is modeled as an asset stage but the current route only returns Topaz process metadata and does not store the final Topaz output.

maintenanceInstructions.purpose: Use this file as the fast orientation map before reading the whole repository.
whenToUpdate: Any route, screen, component, data model, provider integration, environment variable, storage/database schema, test responsibility, or major user flow changes.
Any file is added, removed, renamed, or changes responsibility in a way that affects app architecture.
Any known gap is fixed or a new gap appears.
howToUpdate: Keep this JSON valid and concise.
Prefer factual current-state notes over aspirational descriptions.
Update lastReviewed to the current date when making meaningful architecture changes.
Mention disconnected or partially wired pieces explicitly.
